description: This figure presents a schematic illustration showing the temporal evolution of manganese (Mn) concentration profiles across the ferrite/austenite (α/γ or α/δ/γ) interface during intercritical annealing at 700°C for low-density medium Mn steels (Fe-8Mn-3Al-0.2C and Fe-8Mn-3Al-1Si-0.2C). Panel (a) depicts the initial condition (t=0) when the sample is just brought to the intercritical annealing temperature (IAT), showing uniform Mn concentrations $C_{Mn}^{\gamma}$ in austenite (shaded yellow region) and $C_{Mn}^{\alpha/\delta}$ in ferrite with a sharp discontinuity at the interface. Panel (b) illustrates the intermediate state (t=t1) where local equilibrium establishes at the moving interface, resulting in concentration gradients within both phases: the austenite interface enriches toward the equilibrium concentration $C_{Mn}^{\gamma(eq)}$ while the ferrite side adjusts to $C_{Mn}^{\alpha/\delta(eq)}$. Panel (c) shows the final equilibrium condition (t=∞) with uniform equilibrium concentrations in both phases. The figure demonstrates that during the actual 1-hour annealing process, incomplete Mn diffusion leads to persistent concentration gradients and local Mn enrichment in austenite near the interface, which contributes to higher-than-expected austenite stability and explains discrepancies between equilibrium thermodynamic predictions and experimental retained austenite fractions.
